title: Fix CI build and test execution for release-please pipeline
Formalize domain model types: Session, PhaseInstance, TaskSource, LogEntry
description: A research spike on the broader API surface (pasted into session 2026-06-30) surfaced domain objects beyond our current Phase/Workflow/TimerState: Session, PhaseInstance, TaskSource, TaskQueueItem, LogEntry, NotificationPolicy. Need zod schemas + inferred TS types for these before implementing flow-gu1.7 and flow-gu1.9, so those features build on stable types instead of extending ad-hoc state.
design:
Finalized design (superseding the initial reconciliation notes). Types only in this pass — no wiring into TimerStore/main.ts/timer-view.ts; gu1.7 and gu1.9 wire it up later.

## 1. Zod vs. interface split rule
Zod schema when data crosses a trust boundary: user-authored config (Phase, Workflow, PomodoroSettings — already zod) or values read back from vault frontmatter (currently `unknown`). Plain TS interface/type for records we construct ourselves in memory and never parse from outside input (no validation to do). Session/PhaseInstance/TaskQueueItem -> interfaces. LogEntry -> zod (round-trips through frontmatter). TaskSource -> interface (behavioral contract wrapping live Obsidian objects, not data).

## 2. ID branding
Schema-backed IDs (Phase.id, Workflow.id) use zod's built-in `.brand<'PhaseId'>()` / `.brand<'WorkflowId'>()` — z.infer picks it up for free. Interface-only IDs (SessionId, PhaseInstanceId, TaskQueueItemId) have no schema to brand, so add one generic helper in `src/domain/ids.ts`:
  type Branded<T, B extends string> = T & { readonly __brand: B }
Known wart: two branding mechanisms for one concept. Accepted trade-off — forcing everything through zod just for brand consistency would contradict the interface-for-runtime-only-data rule.

## 3. Phase/Workflow extensions (in place, src/timer/workflow.ts)
- `Phase.kind: PhaseKind` — branded OPEN string (`z.string().min(1).brand<'PhaseKind'>()`), NOT `z.enum([...])`. Cycles may have more than two categories (focus/break is just the built-in case), so kind must not be forced into a fixed set. Ship named constants (`FOCUS_PHASE_KIND`, `BREAK_PHASE_KIND`) for the built-in Pomodoro workflow so timer-view.ts can compare against a constant instead of the current `phase.id.toLowerCase().includes('focus')` substring heuristic.
- `Workflow.taskAdvanceMode: TaskAdvanceMode` — `z.enum(['manual', 'auto']).default('manual')`. Field exists now as a real value; only `'manual'` has a reader. `'auto'` is valid data with no behavior wired yet (gu1.9's job).

## 4. Session / PhaseInstance (new, src/domain/session.ts)
Plain interfaces, nothing constructs them yet.
- `PhaseInstance`: one concrete occurrence of a phase — `id: PhaseInstanceId`, `phaseId: PhaseId`, `plannedDurationSeconds: number`, `actualDurationSeconds: number`, `startedAt: number`, `endedAt: number | null`, `outcome: 'completed' | 'skipped' | 'extended'`.
- `Session`: one workflow traversal — `id: SessionId`, `workflowId: WorkflowId`, `startedAt: number`, `endedAt: number | null`, `activeTaskFilePath: string | null`, `phaseHistory: PhaseInstance[]`.

## 5. TaskSource / TaskQueueItem (new, src/domain/task-source.ts)
- `TaskQueueItem`: `id: TaskQueueItemId`, `filePath: string`, `displayName: string`, `status: 'pending' | 'active' | 'consumed' | 'skipped'`. The `status` field is what gu1.9's auto-advance mode would eventually mutate.
- `TaskSource`: extension-point interface, `getQueue(): TaskQueueItem[]`. Today's inline Bases-entry filtering in timer-view.ts is the implicit default; a real `BaseQuerySource` implementation is deferred to gu1.9.

## 6. LogEntry (new, src/domain/log-entry.ts)
Zod schema: `{ property: z.string().min(1), value: z.union([z.number(), z.string(), z.boolean()]), recordedAt: z.number() }`. Replaces the ad hoc `typeof current === 'number'` check in main.ts#handlePhaseComplete once gu1.7 wires it up (wiring out of scope here).

## 7. NotificationPolicy (new, src/domain/notification-policy.ts)
Deliberate stub, plain interface only: `{ sound: string | null, systemNotification: boolean }`. Upgradeable to zod later if/when it joins persisted settings.

## File layout
New `src/domain/` folder: `ids.ts`, `session.ts`, `task-source.ts`, `log-entry.ts`, `notification-policy.ts`. `src/timer/workflow.ts` extended in place (Phase.kind, Workflow.taskAdvanceMode, brand() on existing id fields) rather than moved.
acceptance_criteria:
- src/timer/workflow.ts: Phase.id/Workflow.id use zod .brand(); Phase gains kind (open branded string, not enum) with FOCUS_PHASE_KIND/BREAK_PHASE_KIND constants; Phase.duration and Workflow.taskAdvanceMode (enum, default manual) added.
- All durations are Temporal.Duration (src/domain/duration.ts: PositiveDurationSchema/NonNegativeDurationSchema); all instants are Temporal.Instant. Phase.durationSeconds/TimerState.remainingSeconds retrofitted accordingly (reducer.ts tick loop, timer-view.ts mm:ss display).
- src/domain/session.ts: Session and PhaseInstance interfaces (plain TS, no zod), durations/instants as Temporal types.
- src/domain/task-source.ts: TaskQueueItem interface + TaskSource extension-point interface (no BaseQuerySource impl yet; deliberately source-agnostic per note that other task sources may exist later).
- src/domain/log-entry.ts: LogEntrySchema (zod, .readonly()) + inferred LogEntry type.
- src/domain/notification-policy.ts: NotificationPolicy stub interface.
- eslint.config.mts: functional/type-declaration-immutability tuned (ignoreIdentifierPattern for Id/Kind, ReadonlyShallow tier for PhaseInstance/Session/TaskQueueItem) to accommodate zod brand's non-readonly tag, documented inline.
- bun run typecheck / lint / test all clean (17 tests passing).
- flow-gu1.7 and flow-gu1.9 can proceed using these types without further modeling detours.
notes:
Source: research spike pasted into agent session on 2026-06-30, covering competitive landscape (eatgrass/obsidian-pomodoro-timer, Flexible Pomodoro) and product naming (Cadence, Phasewright, etc. — naming not in scope for this bead).
Implementation deviated from the initial design in two documented ways, both per explicit direction mid-session:

1. All durations are Temporal.Duration, not number/durationSeconds. This expanded scope beyond "types only": Phase.durationSeconds -> Phase.duration (Temporal.Duration, src/timer/workflow.ts), TimerState.remainingSeconds -> TimerState.remaining (Temporal.Duration, src/timer/reducer.ts), and the tick loop / timer-view.ts mm:ss display were rewritten to operate on Temporal.Duration (via .sign, .subtract(), .total({unit:'seconds'})). PhaseInstance.plannedDuration/actualDuration also Temporal.Duration. All instants (Session/PhaseInstance startedAt/endedAt, LogEntry.recordedAt) are Temporal.Instant. New src/domain/duration.ts holds PositiveDurationSchema/NonNegativeDurationSchema (zod .refine on .sign).

2. No src/domain/ids.ts. Each branded id (SessionId, PhaseInstanceId, TaskQueueItemId) is defined next to its owning file using zod's .brand(), matching the existing PhaseId/WorkflowId pattern in workflow.ts, instead of a hand-rolled Branded<T,B> helper — avoids two branding mechanisms.

Tooling note: zod v4's .brand() tags a type's output with a non-readonly marker property, which trips this repo's functional/type-declaration-immutability (ReadonlyDeep required). Fixed in eslint.config.mts: ignoreIdentifierPattern for id/kind-suffixed identifiers, plus a named ReadonlyShallow tier for PhaseInstance/Session/TaskQueueItem (the aggregates that embed a branded id). zod's own .readonly() was tried first and rejected — it corrupts primitive branded types (Readonly<string & brand> stops being assignable to string) — only used on the LogEntry object schema, where it's actually correct.

Verified: bun run typecheck, bun run lint, bun run test (17 pass) all clean.
close_reason: Domain types formalized and verified (typecheck/lint/test clean). Unblocks flow-gu1.7 and flow-gu1.9.

close_reason: Dynamic task queue filtering by active phase query merged to main in #8 (304f500).

title: State machine for generic phase progression loop
close_reason: Implemented generic Workflow and Phase types, rewrote state machine/reducer/store to be workflow-driven, updated all tests, settings, and main lifecycle hooks.

title: Extensible Pomodoro Timer with Obsidian Bases
Wire up beads Dolt remote and git hooks for durable sync
description: bd dolt remote list shows no remotes configured and sync.remote is unset in .beads/config.yaml — bd init never wired the git origin as the Dolt remote, so refs/dolt/data has never been pushed. There is currently no durable cross-machine sync; only the JSONL export/import fallback exists, and per docs/SYNC_CONCEPTS.md that fallback is explicitly 'not durable sync'. Additionally, none of .beads/hooks/* (pre-commit, post-merge, post-checkout, pre-push, prepare-commit-msg) are installed into .git/hooks/ — Husky only manages pre-commit for lint-staged, not bd export. So nothing currently keeps the local Dolt DB and .beads/issues.jsonl in sync across commits, merges, or checkouts.
design:
Per https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/SYNC_CONCEPTS.md:
1. Run 'bd dolt remote add origin <git-origin-url>' (git+ssh or git+https form) then 'bd dolt push' to publish refs/dolt/data and persist sync.remote into .beads/config.yaml. Commit and push that config change.
2. Install the .beads/hooks/* templates into .git/hooks/ (or integrate their behavior into the existing Husky pre-commit script) so pre-commit keeps issues.jsonl current and post-merge/post-checkout stay in sync with sync.remote configured (avoiding the 'not durable sync' JSONL-import fallback warning).
3. Verify with a second clone / bd bootstrap that a fresh checkout can rehydrate the Dolt DB correctly.
acceptance_criteria:
- bd dolt remote list shows origin configured
- sync.remote is set in .beads/config.yaml (committed)
- refs/dolt/data exists on the git remote after bd dolt push
- .git/hooks/pre-commit, post-merge, post-checkout invoke beads (directly or via Husky) so the JSONL export and local Dolt DB stay in sync without manual bd export/import
- A fresh clone + bd bootstrap successfully rehydrates the issue database
notes: Surfaced while reviewing the (now-deleted) chore/ignore-beads branch, which incorrectly proposed gitignoring all of .beads/ including issues.jsonl/config.yaml/hooks — that branch was closed without merging since it contradicted the documented setup.

title: Status bar and workspace-wide view panel
Custom input modal for write-back forms
Remove Husky, migrate to global/user git hooks
description: Repo currently uses Husky (.husky/pre-commit running lint-staged) for git hook management. Direction is to move away from per-repo Husky towards global/user-level git hooks (e.g. git config --global core.hooksPath pointing at a shared hooks directory, or per-user hook setup) instead. Not urgent — noted while wiring up beads' own hook integration (flow-n1n), where Husky and bd's hooksPath-based install were found to conflict (only one can own core.hooksPath at a time).
notes: Filed 2026-07-01 per explicit direction: 'i dont want husky imo we are transitioning towards global hooks using git or user hooks but whatever its fine for now.' Low priority / no urgency.
